runs.label: A — first ledger generation, no prior entry
runs.text: This section of the commonplace book is organized almost entirely around a single extended conversation with Karpathy (via Dwarkesh Patel) on two related ideas: LLM culture as accumulated, transmissible practice, and self-play as an untapped training paradigm. What's notable is that the collector is not just gathering Karpathy's claims but actively triangulating them against their own experiments — the Claude-log prompt, the multi-pass audit method — treating the collection itself as a kind of proof-of-concept for the culture thesis. The fish tank note is the one genuine outlier, unconnected to the rest.

runs.label: B — same eleven fragments, with the entry from A as prior
D — eleven fragments + two off-topic (basil + tire pressure)
